title: Toasted Tender Peruvian Boiled Rice Pilaf
description: A buttery, crumbly Peruvian dish featuring basmati rice, a perfect fit for a Buddhist temple meal. Cooked in a traditional clay tajine vessel.
instructions: In a traditional clay tajine vessel, heat 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and coriander seeds, and let them sizzle for 1 minute.
Add basmati rice and stir to coat with butter and spices.
Pour in chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes, or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Stir in salt, black pepper, and chopped cilantro.
Increase heat to medium, and add remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter.
Stir constantly until butter has melted and rice is toasted and crumbly.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
